note: เนื้อหาทั้งหมดนี้ AI ร่างขึ้น (Claude) — ต้องให้คุณครูตรวจสอบความถูกต้องของบทอ่าน คำถาม เฉลย และคำอธิบายก่อนนำไปใช้จริงกับนักเรียน เมื่อตรวจแล้วให้เปลี่ยน verified เป็น true

passages.title: A Teenager's Simple Idea Brings Clean Water to a Village
passages.passage:
Nong, a 17-year-old student in northern Thailand, noticed that families in her village often became sick from dirty water. The nearest clean water source was far away, and bottled water was too expensive for many households.

Determined to help, she spent months reading about how to remove dirt and bacteria from water. With guidance from her science teacher, she built a simple filter using sand, small stones, and a layer of charcoal. The materials were cheap and easy to find.

Her device was tested by the local hospital, which confirmed that the filtered water was safe to drink. Soon, neighbours began asking Nong to help them build their own filters.

Today, more than thirty families in the village use her design. Nong hopes to share the idea with other communities that face the same problem. "I am just one student," she said, "but a small idea can change many lives."

questions.question: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: A student builds a cheap water filter to help her village
A hospital checks the drinking water used by a whole town
A family finds that bottled water is too costly to keep buying
A science teacher invents a brand-new water-cleaning machine
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ทั้งบทเล่าเรื่องนักเรียนที่สร้างเครื่องกรองน้ำราคาถูกเพื่อช่วยหมู่บ้าน ส่วน B, C, D เป็นรายละเอียดประกอบ (โรงพยาบาล ราคาน้ำขวด และครู ถูกกล่าวถึงเพียงเล็กน้อย) ไม่ใช่ใจความหลัก

questions.question: What materials did Nong use to build her filter?
choices: Plastic bottles, old cloth, and metal screws
Sand, small stones, and a layer of charcoal
Metal pipes, glass jars, and rubber tubing
Salt, boiling water, and a paper towel
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 ระบุชัดว่าใช้ "sand, small stones, and a layer of charcoal" ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ได้กล่าวถึงในบท จึงเป็นตัวลวง

questions.question: In paragraph 2, the word "Determined" is closest in meaning to:
choices: easily confused
firmly decided
deeply afraid
rather tired
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "determined" แปลว่า มุ่งมั่น/ตั้งใจแน่วแน่ ซึ่งตรงกับ "firmly decided" บริบทคือเธอมุ่งมั่นจะช่วย จึงใช้เวลาหลายเดือนศึกษา ไม่ใช่สับสน กลัว หรือเหนื่อย

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "which" refers to:
choices: the whole village
the filtered water
the local hospital
Nong's new device
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ประโยค "tested by the local hospital, which confirmed..." คำว่า which แทน the local hospital (โรงพยาบาลเป็นผู้ยืนยันว่าน้ำปลอดภัย) ไม่ใช่หมู่บ้าน น้ำ หรือเครื่องกรอง

questions.question: What can be inferred about Nong?
choices: She hopes to become a doctor in the city one day.
She cares about helping others, not only herself.
She wants to sell her filters for a large profit.
She no longer enjoys living in her small village.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — เธอช่วยเพื่อนบ้านสร้างเครื่องกรองและอยากแบ่งปันแนวคิดให้ชุมชนอื่น แสดงว่าใส่ใจช่วยเหลือผู้อื่น ส่วน A, C, D ไม่มีข้อมูลสนับสนุนในบท

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the passage?
choices: To warn readers about the dangers of dirty water
To advertise a new water filter that is for sale
To share an inspiring story about a student's idea
To explain the science of removing harmful bacteria
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทนี้เป็นข่าว/เรื่องเล่าเชิงให้กำลังใจเกี่ยวกับแนวคิดของนักเรียน ไม่ได้มุ่งเตือนภัย ขายสินค้า หรืออธิบายหลักวิทยาศาสตร์อย่างละเอียด

passages.title: App Review: Is 'WordPath' Worth Your Time?
passages.passage:
If you want to grow your English vocabulary, the free app WordPath might be a good place to start. I have used it for two months, and overall I am impressed, though it is not perfect.

The best feature is its daily practice. Each day, the app gives you ten new words with clear examples and a short quiz. The words are useful for real conversations, not just rare words you will never say.

The design is clean and easy to use, even for beginners. However, the app does not work without an internet connection, which is annoying when you are travelling. I also wish it included more listening exercises.

WordPath is not the most advanced app available, but for students who want a simple daily habit, it is an excellent choice. I would recommend it to anyone starting their vocabulary journey.

questions.question: What is the writer's main purpose?
choices: To compare WordPath carefully with several other apps
To give an opinion on whether WordPath is worth using
To explain the best way to study English grammar rules
To complain about an app with a confusing, poor design
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทนี้เป็นรีวิวที่ให้ความเห็นว่าแอปคุ้มค่าจะใช้หรือไม่ ไม่ได้เปรียบเทียบกับแอปอื่น สอนไวยากรณ์ หรือเป็นการบ่นล้วนๆ (ผู้เขียนชมเป็นส่วนใหญ่)

questions.question: According to the review, what is the best feature of WordPath?
choices: Its wide range of useful listening exercises
Its daily practice with ten useful new words
Its ability to work fully without the internet
Its detailed lessons on advanced grammar rules
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่า "The best feature is its daily practice" ส่วน listening กลับเป็นสิ่งที่ผู้เขียน "อยากให้มีมากกว่านี้" และแอปใช้ออฟไลน์ไม่ได้ จึงเป็นตัวลวง

questions.question: What does the writer dislike about the app?
choices: The monthly subscription price is far too high.
It does not work without an internet connection.
The words it teaches are too rare to be useful.
The overall design confuses most new beginners.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 ระบุว่าแอป "does not work without an internet connection, which is annoying" ส่วนแอปนี้ฟรี คำศัพท์มีประโยชน์ และดีไซน์ใช้ง่าย จึงตัดข้ออื่นออก

questions.question: How would you best describe the writer's overall opinion?
choices: Mostly positive, with a few small complaints
Completely negative and quite dismissive
Confused, uncertain, and hard to follow
Angry, harsh, and clearly disappointed
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — แม้จะมีข้อติเล็กน้อย (ออฟไลน์ไม่ได้, อยากได้ listening เพิ่ม) แต่ผู้เขียนชมว่า impressed, excellent choice และ recommend จึงเป็นความเห็นเชิงบวกเป็นส่วนใหญ่

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "annoying" is closest in meaning to:
choices: rather exciting
mildly irritating
quite dangerous
truly helpful
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "annoying" แปลว่า น่ารำคาญ ตรงกับ "slightly irritating" บริบทคือการใช้ออฟไลน์ไม่ได้สร้างความรำคาญเวลาเดินทาง ไม่ใช่ตื่นเต้น อันตราย หรือมีประโยชน์

questions.question: Who would the writer most likely recommend this app to?
choices: An advanced learner who wants rare academic words
A student who wants a simple daily vocabulary habit
A person who has no real interest in learning English
A frequent traveller who is very often left offline
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ผู้เขียนสรุปว่าเหมาะกับ "students who want a simple daily habit" ส่วนผู้ใช้ระดับสูงที่ต้องการคำหายาก และคนที่มักออฟไลน์ จะไม่เหมาะ (แอปเน้นคำใช้บ่อยและต้องต่อเน็ต)

passages.title: Why Do We Dream?
passages.passage:
For as long as people have slept, they have wondered about the strange worlds they visit while dreaming. Ancient cultures often treated dreams as messages from the gods, while today scientists study them in sleep laboratories. Yet despite all this attention, no one can say with complete certainty why we dream. What researchers can offer instead is a set of competing theories, each supported by some evidence.

One widely discussed idea is that dreams help the brain process emotions. During an ordinary day we experience many feelings, and we do not always deal with them fully while we are awake. According to this theory, the sleeping brain returns to these unfinished emotions, replaying and reorganising them. This may be why a worry that seemed enormous late at night can feel far more manageable the following morning.

A second theory connects dreaming with memory. While we sleep, the brain appears to review the information gathered during the day, strengthening the connections that matter and discarding those that do not. Dreams, in this view, are a side effect of that nightly sorting process. The idea fits a familiar piece of advice: students who sleep well after studying tend to remember more than those who stay awake all night.

Not every scientist is convinced that dreams mean anything at all. Some argue that they are simply random signals produced by a resting brain, which then invents a loose story to make sense of the noise. Supporters of this view point out that many dreams are confused, illogical, and impossible to interpret. Whatever the answer finally turns out to be, dreams remain one of the most ordinary and yet most mysterious parts of human life.

questions.question: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: How ancient cultures used dreams to predict the future.
Why students should always sleep well before any exam.
Different scientific theories about why people dream.
A new machine that records dreams in a laboratory.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทเสนอหลายทฤษฎีทางวิทยาศาสตร์ (อารมณ์ / ความจำ / สัญญาณสุ่ม) ว่าทำไมคนจึงฝัน ส่วน A/B/D เป็นรายละเอียดเล็กน้อยหรือไม่ใช่ใจความหลัก

questions.question: According to the emotion theory, what does the sleeping brain do?
choices: It returns to and reorganises unfinished feelings.
It removes every single memory of the previous day.
It produces only random and meaningless signals.
It stops working completely for several hours.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่าสมองขณะหลับ "returns to these unfinished emotions, replaying and reorganising them" จึงตอบข้อ A ส่วนข้ออื่นเป็นทฤษฎีคนละแบบหรือผิด

questions.question: What does the memory theory suggest about dreams?
choices: Dreams are messages sent directly from the gods.
Dreams erase all connections built during the day.
Dreams have no link to learning or studying at all.
Dreams are a side effect of the brain sorting memories.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 อธิบายว่าสมองทบทวนข้อมูลตอนหลับ และความฝันเป็น "a side effect of that nightly sorting process" ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับทฤษฎีนี้

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "discarding" is closest in meaning to:
choices: gathering up
throwing away
writing down
holding tight
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "discard" แปลว่า ทิ้ง/กำจัด ตรงกับ "throwing away" บริบทคือสมองเก็บการเชื่อมโยงที่สำคัญและทิ้งสิ่งที่ไม่สำคัญ

questions.question: What can be inferred from the final paragraph?
choices: Every scientist agrees that dreams carry deep meaning.
All dreams can be clearly and easily interpreted.
There is no single explanation accepted by everyone.
Random signals can never be studied by scientists.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าสุดท้ายขึ้นต้นว่า "Not every scientist is convinced" และเสนอมุมที่ต่างออกไป จึงอนุมานได้ว่ายังไม่มีคำอธิบายเดียวที่ทุกคนยอมรับ

questions.question: In paragraph 2, "these unfinished emotions" refers to feelings that:
choices: we did not fully deal with while awake
appear only inside our strangest dreams
are caused only by studying very hard
scientists create in sleep laboratories
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประโยคก่อนหน้าบอกว่าเราไม่ได้จัดการอารมณ์ให้จบขณะตื่น ("we do not always deal with them fully while we are awake") จึงหมายถึงอารมณ์ที่ยังค้างจากตอนตื่น

questions.question: What is the writer's tone in the passage?
choices: Certain that the mystery has been fully solved.
Angry at scientists for failing to ever agree.
Sad that dreams can never be understood at all.
Curious and open about an unsolved puzzle.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ผู้เขียนเสนอหลายทฤษฎีอย่างเปิดกว้าง ยอมรับว่ายังไม่มีข้อสรุป และเรียกความฝันว่า "most mysterious" จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงใคร่รู้และเปิดกว้าง

questions.question: How is the passage mainly organized?
choices: By telling one long dream from start to finish.
By presenting several competing theories in turn.
By giving step-by-step instructions for sleeping.
By comparing two famous scientists' private lives.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทเรียงโดยเสนอทฤษฎีทีละข้อ (One idea... A second theory... Not every scientist) แล้วชั่งน้ำหนักกัน ไม่ใช่เล่าความฝัน ให้ขั้นตอน หรือเปรียบเทียบชีวิตนักวิทยาศาสตร์

passages.title: The Hidden Cost of Fast Fashion
passages.passage:
Walk into almost any shopping centre today and you will find clothing that is cheaper, more fashionable, and more quickly produced than ever before. This is the world of fast fashion: new styles appear in the shops every few weeks, prices stay remarkably low, and many shoppers buy far more than they could ever actually wear. The system seems to benefit everyone — until we look more closely at what those low prices really hide.

The first hidden cost is environmental. Producing clothing on this enormous scale uses vast quantities of water and energy, and the dyeing of fabrics pollutes rivers in many parts of the world. Worse still, garments are now thrown away faster than ever before. When unwanted clothes reach landfill sites, the synthetic fibres in them can take decades, or even centuries, to break down. The fashion industry has quietly become one of the planet's largest sources of waste.

There is also a human cost that shoppers rarely see. To keep prices as low as possible, some brands depend on factories where workers labour for long hours, earn very little, and face conditions that would be unacceptable elsewhere. Because these workers live far from the shops that sell the clothes, it is easy for customers to forget that a real person, somewhere, paid a price for a cheap shirt.

Recognising these problems does not mean we must stop buying clothes altogether, which would be neither realistic nor fair to the people the industry employs. The more useful response is to buy more thoughtfully. Choosing fewer but better-made items, repairing clothes instead of replacing them, and supporting brands that treat workers and the planet with respect can all make a genuine difference. In the end, the question is not only whether we can afford fast fashion, but whether the planet, and the people who make our clothes, can afford it too.

questions.question: Which statement best expresses the main idea of the passage?
choices: Fast fashion produces brand-new clothing styles every week.
Cheap fast fashion hides real environmental and human costs.
Synthetic fibres are the only cause of pollution on Earth.
Everyone should immediately stop buying any new clothes.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ใจความหลักคือ fast fashion ถูกแต่แฝงต้นทุนทั้งสิ่งแวดล้อมและมนุษย์ ส่วน A เป็นรายละเอียด, C เกินจริง, D ขัดกับย่อหน้าสุดท้าย

questions.question: Why are synthetic fibres described as an environmental problem?
choices: They are far more expensive than natural fibres are.
They cannot be produced quickly enough for the shops.
They are too difficult for factory workers to handle.
They can take decades or centuries to break down.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 ระบุว่าเส้นใยสังเคราะห์ "can take decades, or even centuries, to break down" จึงเป็นปัญหาสิ่งแวดล้อม ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ใช่เหตุผลในบท

questions.question: According to paragraph 3, how do some brands keep prices low?
choices: By relying on factories with long hours and poor conditions.
By selling their clothes only in distant foreign countries.
By using the most expensive natural materials available.
By producing very small quantities of each clothing item.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 บอกว่าบางแบรนด์พึ่งโรงงานที่คนงาน "labour for long hours, earn very little, and face conditions that would be unacceptable" จึงกดราคาได้

questions.question: In paragraph 2, the word "vast" is closest in meaning to:
choices: remarkably cheap
perfectly clean
extremely large
surprisingly rare
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — "vast" แปลว่า มหาศาล/ใหญ่มาก ตรงกับ "extremely large" บริบทคือการผลิตเสื้อผ้าใช้น้ำและพลังงานปริมาณมหาศาล

questions.question: In paragraph 3, "these workers" refers to people who:
choices: buy bargain shirts in large shopping centres
labour long hours in clothing factories
design each one of the brand-new styles
own the large international clothing brands
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ประโยคก่อนหน้ากล่าวถึง "factories where workers labour for long hours" ดังนั้น these workers จึงหมายถึงคนงานในโรงงานเหล่านั้น

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about most shoppers?
choices: They refuse to buy any cheap clothing at all.
They personally know the workers who make clothes.
They rarely think about who makes their clothes.
They repair old clothes instead of buying new ones.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 บอกว่าลูกค้ามักลืมไปว่ามีคนจริงๆ ที่จ่ายราคาให้เสื้อราคาถูก ("easy for customers to forget") จึงอนุมานได้ว่าส่วนใหญ่ไม่ค่อยคิดถึงคนผลิต

questions.question: What is the writer's main purpose?
choices: To advertise one fashionable clothing brand.
To explain exactly how factories sew clothes.
To describe the long history of world fashion.
To make readers weigh the true cost of fast fashion.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — บทเป็นบทความเชิงโน้มน้าวให้ผู้อ่านชั่งน้ำหนักต้นทุนที่แท้จริงของ fast fashion และเสนอทางเลือก ไม่ได้โฆษณา อธิบายการเย็บผ้า หรือเล่าประวัติ

questions.question: How is the passage mainly organized?
choices: It presents problems and then suggests some solutions.
It tells one shopper's story from start to finish.
It compares two rival clothing companies in detail.
It lists fashion events in the order they happened.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — บทเสนอปัญหา (สิ่งแวดล้อม + มนุษย์) แล้วปิดท้ายด้วยทางออกที่ทำได้จริง จึงเป็นโครงแบบปัญหา–ทางแก้ ไม่ใช่เล่าเรื่องหรือเรียงตามเวลา

passages.title: The Paradox of Choice
passages.passage:
Modern life offers more choices than any previous generation could have imagined. A single supermarket may stock forty kinds of breakfast cereal, while an online store can present thousands of options for something as ordinary as a pair of headphones. We generally treat this abundance as a clear sign of progress, assuming without question that more choice must always mean greater freedom and greater happiness. Psychologists, however, have begun to challenge that comfortable assumption.

In principle, a wider range of options should benefit everyone. With more to choose from, each person ought to be able to find the product, the job, or the lifestyle that suits them most precisely. A shop that offers only two kinds of shoes cannot satisfy as many people as one that offers two hundred. On paper, then, choice and satisfaction should rise together, hand in hand.

In practice, the relationship is far more complicated. Once the number of options grows beyond a certain point, many people begin to feel not liberated but overwhelmed. Faced with dozens of broadly similar alternatives, they hesitate, postpone the decision, or occasionally avoid choosing at all. Researchers have even found that shoppers shown a huge display of products are sometimes less likely to buy anything than those offered a small, manageable selection. The very abundance that was meant to help can quietly paralyse.

The difficulty does not end once a decision is finally made. When only one or two options were available, we rarely waste energy wondering about the path we did not take. But when we have rejected twenty attractive alternatives, it becomes tempting to imagine that one of them might have suited us better. This lingering sense of doubt can slowly erode the satisfaction we ought to feel, leaving us faintly disappointed with a perfectly good choice.

None of this means that choice is a bad thing. The freedom to choose remains one of the genuine privileges of modern life, and few of us would willingly return to a world of empty shelves. The point is subtler. Beyond a certain threshold, each additional option adds less and less real value, while quietly increasing both the mental effort required and the risk of later regret. Recognising this paradox will not make our decisions for us, but it may encourage us to set sensible limits, simplify where we can, and feel more content with the good choices we have already made.

questions.question: Which statement best expresses the main idea of the essay?
choices: Supermarkets should always stock as many products as they can.
Beyond a point, more choice can lower rather than raise happiness.
People today are far wealthier than any earlier generation was.
Online stores sell much better headphones than physical shops do.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — แก่นของบทคือ "ทางเลือกที่มากเกินจุดหนึ่งอาจลดความสุขแทนที่จะเพิ่ม" (the paradox of choice) ส่วน A/D เป็นรายละเอียดเล็กน้อย และ C ไม่ใช่ประเด็นหลัก

questions.question: According to paragraph 2, why should more options be a good thing?
choices: Because shops can then charge much higher prices for goods.
Because people enjoy spending many hours comparing products.
Because each person can find what suits them most precisely.
Because two kinds of shoes are always better than two hundred.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่าตัวเลือกที่มากขึ้นควรช่วยให้แต่ละคนหาสิ่งที่ "suits them most precisely" ได้ ส่วน D กลับความหมาย และ A/B ไม่ใช่เหตุผลในบท

questions.question: What did researchers find about shoppers facing a huge display of products?
choices: They were sometimes less likely to buy anything at all.
They almost always bought far more than they planned.
They felt completely calm and confident while choosing.
They strongly preferred displays with thousands of items.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 ระบุว่าคนที่เจอสินค้ากองใหญ่ "sometimes less likely to buy anything" เมื่อเทียบกับกลุ่มที่มีตัวเลือกน้อยกว่า ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับบท

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "paralyse" is closest in meaning to:
choices: make someone feel suddenly wealthy
help someone decide very quickly
fill someone with lasting deep joy
leave someone unable to act
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — "paralyse" ในที่นี้หมายถึงทำให้ตัดสินใจไม่ได้/ขยับไม่ออก ตรงกับ "leave someone unable to act" บริบทคือทางเลือกที่ล้นเกินทำให้คนลังเลจนไม่เลือกเลย

questions.question: In paragraph 4, "the path we did not take" refers to:
choices: a road that leads back to empty store shelves
an option that we considered but chose to reject
the single product that we finally decided to buy
a decision that was made for us by somebody else
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บริบทพูดถึงตัวเลือกที่เราปฏิเสธไป ("rejected twenty attractive alternatives") แล้วอดคิดไม่ได้ว่าตัวที่ไม่ได้เลือกอาจดีกว่า จึงหมายถึงตัวเลือกที่เราเลือกที่จะไม่เอา

questions.question: What can be inferred about the writer's view of choice?
choices: The writer believes all choice should be removed.
The writer thinks choice never causes any problem.
The writer values choice but warns of its downside.
The writer prefers a world with empty store shelves.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าสุดท้ายยืนยันว่าเสรีภาพในการเลือกมีค่า ("a genuine privilege") แต่เตือนถึงด้านลบเมื่อมากเกินไป จึงเป็นการเห็นคุณค่าแต่เตือนข้อเสีย

questions.question: How would you best describe the tone of the essay?
choices: Thoughtful, balanced, and quietly persuasive
Angry, bitter, and openly accusing others
Light, playful, and full of silly jokes
Confused, uncertain, and self-contradicting
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ผู้เขียนชั่งน้ำหนักทั้งสองด้านอย่างมีเหตุผลและค่อยๆ โน้มน้าวให้คิด จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงไตร่ตรอง สมดุล ไม่ใช่โกรธ ตลก หรือสับสน

questions.question: How does the writer mainly develop the argument?
choices: By telling one long story about a single shopper.
By listing the prices found in many different shops.
By giving readers step-by-step buying instructions.
By contrasting how choice works in theory and practice.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ผู้เขียนวาง "In principle / On paper" (ทฤษฎี) ไว้คู่กับ "In practice" (ความจริง) แล้วชี้ความต่าง จึงพัฒนาด้วยการเปรียบเทียบทฤษฎีกับสภาพจริง

questions.question: Based on the essay, what might help a person feel more content?
choices: Always seeking out the largest possible range of options.
Setting sensible personal limits on how much to compare.
Refusing to make any important decisions in life at all.
Returning almost every product soon after buying it.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าสุดท้ายแนะนำให้ "set sensible limits, simplify where we can" เพื่อให้พอใจกับการตัดสินใจมากขึ้น ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับข้อเสนอของผู้เขียน

passages.title: Why Time Seems to Speed Up as We Age
passages.passage:
Ask almost anyone over the age of thirty, and they will describe the same strange sensation: time appears to accelerate as the years go by. The long, lazy summers of childhood seem to have lasted forever, yet entire years now vanish almost without notice. A birthday arrives, and it feels as though the previous one was only weeks ago. Curiously, a year is always exactly the same length — 365 days, no more and no less. What changes is not time itself, but the way our minds measure it. For something so universal, the feeling is surprisingly difficult to explain, and researchers have proposed several competing ideas.

The simplest explanation is mathematical, and it is sometimes called the proportional theory. To a five-year-old child, a single year represents one fifth of everything they have ever lived — an immense, unforgettable stretch of time. To a fifty-year-old, that very same year is merely one part in fifty of their experience. Because each new year forms a smaller and smaller fraction of the life already lived, it naturally feels shorter by comparison. By this logic, the months should appear to shrink steadily as we grow older, which is more or less what most people report.

Psychologists, however, offer a second and complementary explanation, one based on memory rather than mathematics. Our sense of how much time has passed depends heavily on how many distinct experiences we can recall from a given period. Childhood and adolescence are crowded with novelty: a first day at school, a first journey abroad, a first close friendship, a first taste of independence. These vivid, first-time experiences leave deep marks in the memory and make the period feel densely packed, and therefore long. Adult life, by contrast, often settles into comfortable routine. One ordinary working week resembles the next so closely that the brain stores few separate memories, and in looking back we find little to hold on to. The months blur together, and the year seems to have quietly slipped away.

Not everyone is convinced that either theory tells the whole story. Some scientists argue that our perception of time is also shaped by attention and emotion: a frightening or exciting moment can stretch in the memory, while a dull afternoon shrinks to almost nothing. Others point out that the feeling varies from one person to another, which suggests there may be no single, universal cause at all.

Whatever the true explanation, the memory theory in particular offers a hopeful and practical lesson. If routine makes time feel short, then deliberately seeking out novelty may help to reverse the effect. Learning an unfamiliar skill, travelling somewhere new, or simply breaking a daily habit all create fresh memories that, in a sense, expand our experience of time. We cannot slow the clock on the wall, but by filling our years with new and meaningful moments, we may at least make them feel fuller — and, perhaps, longer.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: A year becomes physically shorter as a person grows older.
Childhood summers are clearly the happiest period of anyone life.
Several theories try to explain why time feels faster with age.
Scientists have finally solved the mystery of human memory.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ทั้งบทเสนอหลายทฤษฎี (เชิงสัดส่วน + เชิงความจำ + อารมณ์) เพื่ออธิบายว่าทำไมเวลาจึงรู้สึกผ่านเร็วขึ้นเมื่ออายุมากขึ้น ส่วน A ผิด (บทย้ำว่าปีหนึ่งยาวเท่าเดิมเสมอ), B/D เป็นรายละเอียดหรือเกินจริง

questions.question: According to the proportional theory, why does a year feel shorter to an older person?
choices: Older people are generally far busier than young children.
Each year is a smaller fraction of the life already lived.
Older people gradually lose the ability to measure time.
A year contains slightly fewer days as a person ages.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 อธิบายว่าหนึ่งปีของเด็ก 5 ขวบคือ 1 ใน 5 ของชีวิต แต่ของคน 50 ปีคือ 1 ใน 50 จึงเป็นสัดส่วนที่เล็กลงเรื่อยๆ ไม่เกี่ยวกับความยุ่งหรือจำนวนวันที่ลดลง

questions.question: What does the memory theory say about adult life?
choices: Adults deliberately try to forget their daily routines.
Adults experience more first-time events than children.
Adults can remember their own early childhood far more clearly.
Adult routines blur together, leaving few distinct memories.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 ระบุว่าชีวิตผู้ใหญ่เป็นกิจวัตรซ้ำๆ สมองเก็บความทรงจำแยกได้น้อย เดือนต่างๆ จึงกลืนกัน ตรงข้ามกับวัยเด็กที่เต็มไปด้วยเรื่องครั้งแรก

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "novelty" is closest in meaning to:
choices: new and unfamiliar experiences
comfortable and familiar routines
carefully planned paid activities
long and quiet periods of rest
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — "novelty" แปลว่า ความแปลกใหม่ ตรงกับ "new and unfamiliar experiences" บริบทคือวัยเด็กเต็มไปด้วยเรื่องครั้งแรกที่แปลกใหม่ ตรงข้ามกับกิจวัตรเดิมๆ

questions.question: In paragraph 3, "These vivid, first-time experiences" refers to events such as:
choices: the ordinary working weeks of adult life
frightening or very dull adult afternoons
a first day at school or a first journey
mathematical fractions of a whole lifetime
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ประโยคก่อนหน้าได้ยกตัวอย่าง "a first day at school, a first journey abroad, a first close friendship" ดังนั้นวลีนี้จึงหมายถึงเหตุการณ์ครั้งแรกในวัยเด็ก

questions.question: What can be inferred from paragraph 4?
choices: All scientists fully agree on one single cause.
The cause may not be exactly the same for everyone.
Emotion has no real effect on our sense of time.
Children apparently never feel that time is passing quickly at all.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าที่ 4 บอกว่าความรู้สึกนี้ "varies from one person to another" และอาจไม่มีสาเหตุเดียวที่ใช้ได้กับทุกคน จึงอนุมานได้ว่าสาเหตุอาจต่างกันไปในแต่ละคน

questions.question: How would you best describe the writer's tone?
choices: Anxious, gloomy, and rather hopeless
Strongly critical of all the theories
Amused and entirely light-hearted
Curious, balanced, and gently hopeful
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ผู้เขียนเสนอหลายทฤษฎีอย่างเป็นกลาง ใคร่รู้ และปิดท้ายด้วยข้อคิดเชิงบวก ("a hopeful and practical lesson") จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงใคร่รู้ สมดุล และให้ความหวังเล็กน้อย

questions.question: How is the passage mainly organized?
choices: It describes a feeling, explores theories, then gives advice.
It lists historical events in strict chronological order.
It compares the lives of two well-known scientists.
It gives step-by-step instructions for one experiment.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — บทเริ่มด้วยความรู้สึก (เวลาผ่านเร็ว) → สำรวจหลายทฤษฎี → ปิดท้ายด้วยคำแนะนำที่นำไปใช้ได้ (หาประสบการณ์ใหม่) ไม่ใช่เรียงตามเวลา เปรียบเทียบคน หรือให้ขั้นตอนการทดลอง

questions.question: Based on the passage, which action would most likely make a year feel longer?
choices: Following the same fixed routine every single day.
Sleeping a few more hours during the work week.
Travelling somewhere new and learning fresh skills.
Carefully avoiding absolutely any change to one daily habits.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าสุดท้ายเสนอว่าการหาความแปลกใหม่ (เรียนทักษะใหม่ ไปที่ใหม่ เปลี่ยนกิจวัตร) สร้างความทรงจำใหม่และทำให้รู้สึกว่าเวลายาวขึ้น ส่วนการทำกิจวัตรเดิมจะยิ่งทำให้เวลาผ่านเร็ว

passages.title: Email: English Camp Sign-Up
passages.passage:
From: Ms. Pranee Wong, English Club Coordinator
To: All English Club Members
Subject: English Camp — Important Details and Sign-Up

Dear members,

I am pleased to announce that our annual English Camp will take place on Saturday 15 March at the school hall, from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. This year's theme is "Storytelling Through Drama," and we are lucky to have a guest speaker from Khon Kaen University leading the afternoon workshop.

Please note the following points:
- The camp is free, but places are limited to forty students.
- To reserve a place, reply to this email by Friday 7 March.
- Bring a packed lunch; drinks and snacks will be provided.
- Wear comfortable clothes, as some activities involve movement.

If you have already signed up for the debate competition, you may still join the camp, but please tell me in advance so that I can arrange the schedule.

I look forward to seeing many of you there. Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Ms. Pranee Wong
English Club Coordinator

questions.question: What is the main purpose of this email?
choices: To announce an English Camp and explain how to join.
To cancel the school's annual English Camp this year.
To invite members to a debate competition next week.
To report on the results of last year's English Camp.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — อีเมลประกาศการจัด English Camp และบอกวิธีสมัคร (reply by Friday 7 March) ส่วน B/C/D ขัดกับเนื้อหา (ไม่ได้ยกเลิก ไม่ใช่การแข่ง debate และไม่ได้รายงานผลปีก่อน)

questions.question: How can a student reserve a place at the camp?
choices: By paying a small fee at the school office.
By calling the guest speaker before the camp.
By replying to the email before Friday 7 March.
By signing a form at the hall on the camp day.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — อีเมลระบุว่า "To reserve a place, reply to this email by Friday 7 March" ส่วนค่ายนี้ฟรี (ไม่ต้องจ่าย) จึงตัดข้ออื่นออก

questions.question: What are students asked to bring to the camp?
choices: Their own bottles of water and snacks.
A packed lunch for the middle of the day.
A laptop computer for the drama workshop.
A ticket bought from the coordinator first.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — อีเมลบอกว่า "Bring a packed lunch; drinks and snacks will be provided" ดังนั้นต้องเตรียมอาหารกลางวันมาเอง ส่วนเครื่องดื่มและของว่างทางค่ายจัดให้

questions.question: What can be inferred about the English Camp?
choices: It is expected to attract very few students.
It will be held outdoors in the school garden.
It is open only to students who can pay a fee.
It is popular, so places may fill up quickly.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ค่ายฟรีแต่ "places are limited to forty" และให้รีบ reply ภายในวันที่กำหนด จึงอนุมานได้ว่าเป็นที่นิยมและที่นั่งอาจเต็มเร็ว

questions.question: In the email, the word "reserve" is closest in meaning to:
choices: to give back
to pay for
to book ahead
to describe
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — "reserve a place" หมายถึงจองที่ไว้ล่วงหน้า ตรงกับ "to book ahead" ไม่ใช่คืน จ่ายเงิน หรือบรรยาย

questions.question: How would you best describe the tone of the email?
choices: Friendly, clear, and informative
Angry, harsh, and complaining
Sad, gloomy, and apologetic
Cold, distant, and threatening
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ผู้เขียนใช้ภาษาสุภาพเป็นกันเอง (Dear members, I look forward to seeing you, Feel free to contact me) พร้อมให้ข้อมูลชัดเจน จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงเป็นมิตรและให้ข้อมูล

passages.title: Notice: Library Hours During Exams
passages.passage:
NOTICE — School Library

To all students,

During the final examination period (1–14 October), the library will extend its opening hours to help you prepare.

New opening hours:
- Monday to Friday: 7 a.m. – 8 p.m.
- Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
- Sunday: closed

Please remember the following rules:
- Keep your voice low. The study zone on the second floor is for silent study only.
- You may borrow up to five books at a time during this period.
- Food is not allowed inside, but covered water bottles are fine.

Students who return books late will not be able to borrow again until the fine has been paid.

We wish you the very best of luck in your exams.

The Library Team

questions.question: What is the main purpose of this notice?
choices: To announce that the library will close for exams.
To remind students to return all their library books.
To inform students of extended hours during exams.
To introduce a new study zone on the first floor.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ประกาศแจ้งว่าห้องสมุด "extend its opening hours" ช่วงสอบ ส่วน A ตรงข้ามกับเนื้อหา, B/D เป็นรายละเอียดเล็กน้อยหรือไม่ตรง

questions.question: When is the library open on Saturdays during this period?
choices: From 9 a.m. until 5 p.m. in the afternoon.
From 7 a.m. until 8 p.m. in the evening.
It stays completely closed all of Saturday.
From 9 a.m. until 8 p.m. without any break.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประกาศระบุ "Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m." ส่วน 7–20 น. คือจันทร์–ศุกร์ และวันอาทิตย์ปิด จึงตัดข้ออื่นออก

questions.question: What does the notice say about the second-floor study zone?
choices: It is reserved only for teachers during exams.
It is the place where students may eat lunch.
It is closed throughout the whole exam period.
It is for silent study, so voices must be low.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ประกาศบอกว่าโซนชั้นสองเป็น "silent study only" และให้เก็บเสียงเบา ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่มีในประกาศ

questions.question: What will happen to a student who returns books late?
choices: They will be banned from the library forever.
They cannot borrow again until the fine is paid.
They must pay double the price of the books.
They will be reported to their own class teacher.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ประกาศระบุว่าคนคืนหนังสือช้า "will not be able to borrow again until the fine has been paid" ตัวเลือกอื่นเกินจริงหรือไม่มีในบท

questions.question: In the notice, the word "extend" is closest in meaning to:
choices: to cut short
to close down
to make longer
to clean up
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — "extend its hours" หมายถึงขยาย/ทำให้นานขึ้น ตรงกับ "to make longer" ไม่ใช่ตัดให้สั้นหรือปิด

questions.question: How would you describe the tone of the notice?
choices: Helpful and encouraging
Angry and impatient
Sad and apologetic
Funny and playful
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประกาศช่วยอำนวยความสะดวก (ขยายเวลา) และอวยพร "best of luck" จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงช่วยเหลือและให้กำลังใจ

passages.title: How to Set Up Your Student Email Account
passages.passage:
Follow these steps to set up your school email account before the first day of class.

1. Open your web browser and go to the school website.
2. Click the "Student Login" button at the top right of the page.
3. Enter the username printed on your registration card. Your first password is your date of birth in the form DDMMYYYY.
4. The system will then ask you to create a new password. Choose one that is at least eight characters long and includes a number.
5. Once you are logged in, read the "Welcome" message, which contains your class timetable.

If you forget your password, do not create a second account. Instead, visit the IT office in Room 105 with your student card, and a staff member will reset it for you.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of this text?
choices: To explain how to repair a broken school computer.
To explain how to set up a student email account.
To explain how to register for brand-new classes.
To explain how to contact a teacher by email.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ข้อความเป็นขั้นตอนการตั้งค่าอีเมลนักเรียน (set up your school email account) ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงกับเนื้อหา

questions.question: What is a student's first password?
choices: The username printed on the registration card.
A word chosen freely by the student in advance.
A secret code sent later by the IT office staff.
The student's date of birth in DDMMYYYY form.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ขั้นที่ 3 ระบุว่ารหัสผ่านแรกคือวันเกิดรูปแบบ DDMMYYYY ส่วน username ใช้ล็อกอิน ไม่ใช่รหัสผ่าน

questions.question: What must the new password include?
choices: At least eight characters and a number.
At least four letters and one symbol.
The student's full name and birth year.
A capital letter and the word school.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ขั้นที่ 4 บอกว่ารหัสใหม่ต้องยาวอย่างน้อย 8 ตัวอักษรและมีตัวเลข ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงเงื่อนไข

questions.question: What should a student do if they forget the password?
choices: Quickly create a brand-new second account.
Send an email straight to the headmaster.
Visit the IT office with a student card.
Wait for the system to reset it on its own.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ข้อความบอกว่าอย่าสร้างบัญชีที่สอง ให้ไปที่ IT office ห้อง 105 พร้อมบัตรนักเรียน ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับคำแนะนำ

questions.question: In the text, the word "reset" is closest in meaning to:
choices: to delete fully
to set again
to print out
to lock away
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "reset the password" คือตั้งรหัสใหม่ให้ ตรงกับ "to set again" ไม่ใช่ลบทิ้ง พิมพ์ หรือล็อก

questions.question: How is the information mainly organized?
choices: As a list of rules in order of importance.
As a comparison between two email systems.
As a story about one student's first day.
As a series of steps in the order to follow.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ข้อความเรียงเป็นขั้นตอน 1–5 ตามลำดับที่ต้องทำ จึงเป็นการจัดเรียงแบบขั้นตอน ไม่ใช่เปรียบเทียบหรือเล่าเรื่อง

passages.title: The Rise of Urban Farming
passages.passage:
In cities around the world, a quiet change is taking place on rooftops, balconies, and empty plots of land. Urban farming — growing food within a city rather than in the countryside — is becoming increasingly popular, and for several practical reasons.

The first is freshness. Vegetables grown in the city can reach local markets within hours, rather than travelling long distances by truck. This not only improves their taste but also reduces the fuel used in transport.

Urban farms also make use of space that would otherwise be wasted. A flat rooftop, for example, can be turned into a productive garden that helps cool the building below. Some restaurants now grow herbs on their own roofs, picking them only minutes before they are served.

There are social benefits too. Community gardens, where neighbours share a plot of land, bring people together and give city children a chance to see how food is grown. For many families, this connection to nature is a welcome break from busy urban life.

Of course, urban farming cannot replace traditional agriculture; cities simply do not have enough space to feed everyone. But as a supplement, it offers fresher food, greener buildings, and stronger communities — a small but meaningful step toward more sustainable cities.

questions.question: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Why traditional farming is no longer necessary.
How to grow vegetables on a small balcony.
The benefits of farming inside modern cities.
The long history of farming in the countryside.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทเล่าข้อดีของการทำเกษตรในเมือง (urban farming) ส่วน A ขัดกับย่อหน้าสุดท้าย, B/D เป็นรายละเอียดหรือไม่ใช่ใจความหลัก

passages.title: Should Students Use Smartphones in Class?
passages.passage:
Few topics divide teachers and students as sharply as the use of smartphones in the classroom. Some schools ban them completely, while others encourage their use as learning tools. So which side is right?

Those who oppose phones in class make a strong case. Smartphones are a constant source of distraction: a single notification can pull a student's attention away from a lesson, and research suggests that even having a phone on the desk can reduce concentration. There is also the temptation to cheat, or simply to scroll through social media instead of listening.

Supporters, however, argue that banning phones ignores their real value. A smartphone can be a dictionary, a calculator, and a gateway to information all at once. Used well, it allows students to look up unfamiliar words, take part in online quizzes, and learn at their own pace.

The truth, perhaps, lies somewhere in between. The problem is not the device itself but how it is used. Rather than banning phones outright, schools might teach students to use them responsibly — switching them to silent during explanations, and taking them out only for specific tasks. In a world where technology is everywhere, learning to manage it may prove more useful than learning to live without it.

questions.question: What is the main question the writer explores?
choices: Whether smartphones are too costly for students.
Whether teachers should be allowed to use phones.
Whether social media is harmful to young people.
Whether students should use smartphones in class.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ทั้งบทถกประเด็น "ควรให้นักเรียนใช้สมาร์ตโฟนในห้องเรียนไหม" ตัวเลือกอื่นเป็นประเด็นข้างเคียงที่ไม่ใช่คำถามหลัก

choices: Phones are a constant source of distraction.

choices: By replacing the teacher in every single lesson.

questions.question: In paragraph 4, the word "outright" is closest in meaning to:
choices: slowly and carefully

choices: Phones should be banned from every classroom.

choices: Balanced, weighing both sides before judging.

choices: It lists steps for using a phone in class.

passages.title: Letter: A Reply to a Customer Complaint
passages.passage:
Dear Ms. Chen,

Thank you for your letter of 3 May regarding the laptop bag you purchased from our online store. I am sorry to hear that the zip broke after only two weeks, and I understand how frustrating this must be.

Having looked into your case, I can confirm that your purchase is still covered by our one-year warranty. We would therefore like to offer you two options. You may either return the faulty bag for a full refund, or we can send you a replacement free of charge. In both cases, we will cover the cost of postage.

To proceed, simply reply to this letter and let us know which option you prefer. If you choose a replacement, please confirm your delivery address so that we can send the new bag without delay.

Once again, please accept our apologies for the inconvenience. We value you as a customer and hope to serve you better in the future.

Yours sincerely,
David Lim
Customer Service Manager

choices: To advertise a brand-new range of laptop bags.
To respond to a complaint and offer some options.
To ask the customer to pay for a repair service.
To explain why the warranty has already expired.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — จดหมายตอบข้อร้องเรียนเรื่องกระเป๋าเสียและเสนอทางเลือก (คืนเงินหรือเปลี่ยนใหม่) ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับเนื้อหา

choices: A discount voucher or a written apology.

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — จดหมายเสนอ "a full refund" หรือ "a replacement free of charge" ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงกับข้อเสนอ

passages.title: How Music Shapes the Brain
passages.passage:
Most of us listen to music every day — on the way to school, while studying, or simply to relax — without thinking much about what it actually does to us. Yet over the past few decades, scientists have discovered that music is far more than entertainment. It affects the human brain in remarkable ways, shaping our emotions, our memory, our bodies, and even the way we learn.

Perhaps the most obvious effect of music is on our mood. A fast, upbeat song can make us feel energetic, while a slow, gentle melody can calm us down or bring back distant memories. This happens because music triggers the release of chemicals in the brain that are linked to pleasure and reward — the same chemicals involved when we eat a favourite food or receive good news. This is why a single song can sometimes change the way we feel in a matter of seconds.

Music also has a surprisingly powerful connection to memory. Many people find it far easier to remember information when it is set to a tune; this is exactly why young children learn the alphabet through song. Researchers have even found that patients who have lost much of their memory through illness can still recall songs from their youth, long after most other memories have faded. The rhythm and melody seem to provide a kind of map that helps the brain store and later recover information.

Beyond thoughts and feelings, music can even influence the body. Studies have shown that listening to a steady beat can help people walk more smoothly, which is why music is now used to support patients recovering from injuries that affect movement. Hospitals sometimes play calming music to lower patients' stress before an operation, and athletes rely on energetic playlists to push themselves further during training. The body, it seems, responds naturally to rhythm, often without our being aware of it.

Given all these benefits, it is tempting to assume that music always helps us study. The truth, however, is more complicated. Quiet background music without words may help some people concentrate, but loud music with lyrics often does the opposite, competing for the very attention that the task requires. Whether music helps or harms, in other words, depends on the person, the type of music, and the kind of work being done.

What is clear is that music is deeply woven into the way our minds and bodies work. It can lift our spirits, unlock distant memories, ease pain, and, used wisely, support our learning. The next time you put on your headphones, it may be worth remembering that you are not simply hearing a song — you are giving your brain a gentle but genuine workout.

choices: How to choose the best music for studying hard.
The many ways music affects the brain and body.

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทเล่าผลของดนตรีต่อสมองและร่างกายหลายด้าน (อารมณ์ ความจำ ร่างกาย การเรียน) ส่วน A/C/D เป็นรายละเอียดย่อย

passages.title: Should Single-Use Plastics Be Banned?
passages.passage:
Plastic bags, straws, bottles, and food containers have become part of daily life. They are cheap, light, and convenient — and they are almost everywhere. In recent years, however, many governments have begun to ban or tax single-use plastics: items that are designed to be thrown away after only one use. Supporters say such measures are long overdue, while critics argue that they cause more problems than they solve. So which side has the stronger case?

Those in favour of banning single-use plastics point to the enormous damage these products cause. The great majority of them are never recycled. Instead, they end up in rivers, oceans, and landfills, where they can take hundreds of years to break down. Along the way, they harm wildlife: sea turtles mistake floating plastic bags for jellyfish, and seabirds feed tiny plastic fragments to their chicks. Every year, millions of tonnes of plastic flow into the sea, and much of it never truly disappears — it only breaks into smaller and smaller pieces. By cutting plastic off at the source, supporters argue, a ban deals with the problem before it begins, rather than relying on recycling systems that frequently fail.

Opponents, however, raise a number of practical concerns. Plastic is cheap, light, and hygienic, and for some people it is genuinely hard to replace. Disabled people, for instance, may depend on flexible plastic straws that stiff paper ones cannot match. Small businesses worry about the rising cost of alternatives, and some scientists point out that producing paper or cotton bags can actually use more water and energy than making plastic ones. A poorly designed ban, they warn, may simply move the harm somewhere else rather than removing it.

Both sides make fair points, and the issue is rarely as simple as it first appears. A ban that removes plastic straws but ignores the far larger problem of plastic packaging may achieve very little in practice. On the other hand, doing nothing at all is clearly not a serious option, given the sheer scale of plastic pollution today. The wisest path may lie in careful design: banning the most harmful and least necessary items first, while offering affordable alternatives and protecting the small number of people who truly need certain plastic products.

In the end, single-use plastics offer a useful example of how environmental decisions are seldom black and white. Banning them can bring real and lasting benefits, but only if it is done thoughtfully, with proper attention to cost, fairness, and the wider picture. The aim, after all, is not merely to be seen to be doing something, but to make a genuine difference to the planet that we all share.

questions.question: What is the main question the passage explores?
choices: How plastic bottles and bags are made in factories.
Why plastic is cheaper than paper and cotton bags.
Whether single-use plastics should be banned or not.
How sea turtles and seabirds protect their young.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ทั้งบทถกว่าควรแบนพลาสติกใช้ครั้งเดียวหรือไม่ ตัวเลือกอื่นเป็นรายละเอียดย่อยหรือไม่ใช่ประเด็นหลัก

questions.question: What do supporters of a ban say about most single-use plastics?
choices: They are never recycled and last for hundreds of years.
They are cheaper to make than any other material.
They can easily be replaced by paper in almost every case.
They are safely burned without harming the air.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่าพลาสติกส่วนใหญ่ไม่ถูกรีไซเคิลและใช้เวลาย่อยสลายหลายร้อยปี ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ใช่เหตุผลฝ่ายสนับสนุน

questions.question: What practical concern do opponents of a ban raise?
choices: Plastic is more dangerous to wildlife than glass is.
Recycling plastic is cheaper than producing it new.

Some people genuinely need flexible plastic straws.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 ยกตัวอย่างว่าผู้พิการบางคนต้องใช้หลอดพลาสติกที่งอได้ ซึ่งหลอดกระดาษแข็งแทนไม่ได้ ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ใช่ข้อกังวลที่บทกล่าว

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "hygienic" is closest in meaning to:
choices: very expensive
clean and safe
easily broken

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "hygienic" แปลว่า ถูกสุขอนามัย/สะอาดปลอดภัย ตรงกับ "clean and safe"

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about a ban on plastic straws alone?
choices: It would completely solve the plastic problem.
It is the most important step a country can take.
It may do little if larger problems are ignored.
It would be impossible for any government to pass.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 4 บอกว่าการแบนหลอดแต่ละเลยปัญหาบรรจุภัณฑ์ที่ใหญ่กว่าอาจได้ผลน้อย จึงอนุมานได้ว่าแบนเฉพาะหลอดอย่างเดียวช่วยได้ไม่มากถ้ามองข้ามปัญหาใหญ่

questions.question: In paragraph 3, the word "alternatives" refers to:
choices: other materials used in place of plastic
the many different brands of plastic drinking straws
the small businesses that sell goods
laws that ban single-use plastics
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — บริบทพูดถึงต้นทุนของวัสดุทดแทนพลาสติก (กระดาษ/ผ้าฝ้าย) ดังนั้น alternatives จึงหมายถึงวัสดุอื่นที่ใช้แทนพลาสติก

questions.question: How would you best describe the writer's overall position?
choices: Firmly against banning any plastic at all.
Completely certain that all bans are useless.
Uninterested in the issue of pollution.
Balanced, favouring careful and fair bans.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ผู้เขียนเสนอทั้งสองฝ่ายแล้วสรุปว่าควรแบนอย่างรอบคอบและเป็นธรรม จึงเป็นจุดยืนสมดุลที่หนุนการแบนแบบมีการออกแบบที่ดี

questions.question: How is the passage mainly organized?
choices: It lists the steps for recycling plastic at home.
It gives both sides, then suggests a wise middle path.
It tells the story of one plastic bottle's journey.
It compares plastic pollution in two countries.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทเสนอฝ่ายสนับสนุน → ฝ่ายค้าน → แล้วเสนอทางสายกลางที่รอบคอบ จึงเป็นโครงแบบนี้

questions.question: Based on the passage, what would the writer most likely support?
choices: Banning every single type of plastic immediately and completely.
Keeping all single-use plastics exactly as they are.
Banning the most harmful items while offering alternatives.
Leaving the decision entirely to plastic companies.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ผู้เขียนเสนอให้แบนสิ่งที่อันตรายและไม่จำเป็นที่สุดก่อน พร้อมจัดหาทางเลือกที่จับต้องได้ จึงน่าจะสนับสนุนแนวทางนี้

passages.title: Why We Procrastinate
passages.passage:
Almost everyone has done it. A deadline is approaching, an important task is waiting, and yet we find ourselves tidying a desk, checking a phone, or watching just one more video instead. Procrastination — the habit of needlessly delaying what we know we should do — is one of the most common of human weaknesses. What makes it so puzzling is that we often procrastinate even when we fully understand that it will cost us dearly later. Why do intelligent, capable people so regularly act against their own interests?

For a long time, procrastination was dismissed as simple laziness or weak willpower. More recent research, however, suggests something far more interesting. Psychologists now argue that procrastination is less about managing time than about managing emotions. When a task makes us feel anxious, bored, or unsure of ourselves, putting it off offers instant relief. We are not really avoiding the work itself so much as avoiding the unpleasant feelings attached to it.

This helps explain why procrastination can feel so pleasant in the moment and so painful afterwards. By delaying a difficult task, we escape discomfort now, but we quietly hand a larger problem to our future selves. The relief we gain is genuine but temporary; the task does not vanish, and it usually grows more stressful as the deadline draws nearer. In this sense, procrastination behaves like a trap that rewards us immediately while punishing us in the long run.

Part of the explanation lies in the way the human brain weighs time. We tend to value rewards that we can enjoy right now far more highly than benefits that lie somewhere in the future — a tendency that psychologists call "present bias." The discomfort of beginning a hard task is immediate and vivid, while the satisfaction of completing it feels distant and abstract. Faced with this imbalance, the brain naturally leans towards the easier and more comfortable option in front of it.

Seeing procrastination as an emotional habit, rather than a simple character flaw, points the way towards better solutions. Instead of merely ordering ourselves to "try harder," we can make the first step smaller and less frightening — promising to work for just five minutes, for example, or breaking an enormous task into smaller, manageable pieces. Interestingly, research also suggests that forgiving ourselves for past delays makes us less likely to procrastinate again, because it removes some of the very anxiety that caused the problem in the first place.

Procrastination, then, is not really a sign that we are lazy or foolish. It is a deeply human reaction to uncomfortable feelings, and like most habits, it can be understood and slowly changed. The next time you catch yourself delaying, the most useful question may not be "Why am I so lazy?" but rather "What is it about this task that I am trying to avoid?" — and that small shift in thinking can make a surprising difference.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Procrastination is caused mainly by poor time management.
Lazy people are the only ones who ever procrastinate.
Procrastination is an emotional habit that can be changed.
Most tasks are best left until the very last minute.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — แก่นของบทคือ การผัดวันประกันพรุ่งเป็นนิสัยทางอารมณ์ที่เข้าใจและค่อยๆ แก้ได้ ส่วน A/B ขัดกับงานวิจัยที่บทเสนอ และ D ไม่ใช่สาระ

questions.question: According to recent research, what is procrastination really about?
choices: Managing uncomfortable emotions rather than time.
Carefully planning every hour of the working day.
A lack of intelligence in otherwise capable people.
Choosing the hardest possible task to do first.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่างานวิจัยใหม่มองว่าการผัดงานเป็นเรื่องการจัดการ "อารมณ์" มากกว่าการจัดการเวลา

questions.question: Why does the writer compare procrastination to a trap?
choices: Because it is impossible for anyone to ever escape it.
Because it punishes us the moment we begin a task.
Because it slowly makes every task more enjoyable.
Because it rewards us now but punishes us later.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 บอกว่าการผัดงานให้ความสุขทันทีแต่สร้างปัญหาที่ใหญ่ขึ้นในอนาคต จึงเหมือนกับดักที่ให้รางวัลตอนนี้แต่ลงโทษทีหลัง

questions.question: In paragraph 4, the phrase "present bias" refers to the tendency to:
choices: value future rewards more than immediate ones
value present rewards more than future ones
ignore both present and future rewards equally
treat present and future rewards as the same
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทอธิบายว่า present bias คือการให้ค่ารางวัลที่ได้ตอนนี้มากกว่ารางวัลในอนาคต จึงตรงกับข้อ B

questions.question: What can be inferred about starting a hard task?
choices: It always feels easy once the deadline is close.
It brings an immediate and lasting sense of joy.
Its discomfort feels stronger than its distant reward.
It is impossible unless someone forces us to begin.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 4 บอกว่าความอึดอัดตอนเริ่มงานนั้นชัดและทันที ขณะที่รางวัลตอนทำเสร็จดูไกลและเป็นนามธรรม จึงอนุมานได้ว่าความอึดอัดรู้สึกแรงกว่ารางวัลที่อยู่ไกล

questions.question: What does research say about forgiving ourselves for past delays?
choices: It makes us less likely to procrastinate again.
It encourages us to delay even more in future.
It has no measurable effect on our behaviour.
It works only for people who are naturally lazy.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 5 ระบุว่าการให้อภัยตัวเองเรื่องการผัดงานในอดีตช่วยลดโอกาสผัดงานซ้ำ เพราะลดความวิตกที่เป็นต้นเหตุ

questions.question: In paragraph 5, the word "manageable" is closest in meaning to:
choices: extremely difficult to finish
completely impossible to start
far too large to even attempt
easy enough to deal with
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — "manageable pieces" หมายถึงชิ้นงานที่จัดการไหว ตรงกับ "easy enough to deal with" บริบทคือแบ่งงานใหญ่เป็นส่วนเล็กที่รับมือได้

questions.question: How would you best describe the writer's tone?
choices: Harsh and strongly critical of lazy people.
Understanding, thoughtful, and encouraging.
Confused and unsure about the whole topic.
Amused, joking, and not at all serious.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ผู้เขียนอธิบายอย่างเข้าอกเข้าใจ ไม่ตัดสินว่าขี้เกียจ และเสนอทางออกเชิงให้กำลังใจ จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงเข้าใจและให้กำลังใจ

questions.question: How is the passage mainly organized?
choices: It tells a single person's story in time order.
It lists steps for finishing tasks in strict order.
It explains a problem, its causes, then solutions.
It compares two famous psychologists' careers.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทเริ่มด้วยปัญหา (ทำไมเราผัดงาน) → อธิบายสาเหตุ (อารมณ์ + present bias) → เสนอทางแก้ จึงเป็นโครงปัญหา–สาเหตุ–ทางออก
